story.iWant: to connect my Google Calendar account via OAuth 2.0
story.soThat: the app can securely access my calendar data and maintain my credentials persistently across sessions
story.tasks: - Task 1: Add NuGet packages (Google.Apis.Calendar.v3 1.73.0.3993, Google.Apis.Auth 1.73.0.x, Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Polly 9.0.x, CommunityToolkit.Mvvm 8.x) - Task 2: Create OperationResult<T> shared record in Services/OperationResult.cs - Task 3: Implement ITokenStorageService and DpapiTokenStorageService (DPAPI encrypt/decrypt, Config table storage) - Task 4: Create IGoogleCalendarService interface and GoogleCalendarService (auth portion only; stub FetchAll/Incremental) - Task 5: Create SettingsViewModel with ConnectGoogleCalendarCommand and DisconnectGoogleCalendarCommand - Task 6: Update SettingsPage.xaml with Connect/Reconnect buttons and status text - Task 7: Add credentials folder setup and .gitignore / README update - Task 8: Write unit tests in GoogleCalendarManagement.Tests/Unit/AuthenticationTests.cs - Task 9: Final validation (build, tests, manual OAuth flow)

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Given the app has no stored credentials, when the user clicks "Connect Google Calendar" in Settings, the system browser opens to Google's OAuth consent screen.

Given the user completes OAuth consent, the app receives access and refresh tokens, encrypts them with Windows DPAPI (ProtectedData.Protect, DataProtectionScope.CurrentUser), and stores the Base64-encoded ciphertext in the Config table (ConfigKey = "GcalTokenResponse") — no plaintext credentials persist on disk.

Given a stored refresh token in the Config table, when the app starts, GoogleCalendarService.IsAuthenticatedAsync() silently loads and validates the token without user interaction. Must complete in < 200ms.
Given a valid access token that expires mid-session, the Google SDK credential object automatically refreshes it before the next API call with no user-visible interruption.

Given the user clicks "Reconnect Google Calendar" in Settings, RevokeAndClearTokensAsync() clears the Config row (ConfigKey = "GcalTokenResponse") and a new OAuth flow begins, supporting account switching.

Given authentication fails (network error, user cancels, invalid credentials), a user-friendly error message is displayed (no stack trace), OperationResult.Success = false is returned, and no partial token state is persisted.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification: Google Calendar Integration & Sync (Read-Only)
doc.snippet: Authoritative acceptance criteria for Story 2.1. Defines the full OAuth flow: GoogleWebAuthorizationBroker.AuthorizeAsync → DpapiTokenStorageService.StoreTokenAsync → AppMetadata upsert → WeakReferenceMessenger.Send(AuthenticationSucceededMessage). Note: "AppMetadata" in the tech spec maps to the Config entity in the actual codebase.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: TokenResponse JSON serialized and encrypted with ProtectedData.Protect(data, null, DataProtectionScope.CurrentUser) before storage. OAuth scope requested: https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar (read+write) — pre-provisioned for Epic 6. client_secret.json must not be committed to source control; loaded from %LOCALAPPDATA%\GoogleCalendarManagement\credentials\client_secret.json.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: User clicks "Connect Google Calendar" → SettingsViewModel.ConnectGoogleCalendarAsync() → GoogleCalendarService.AuthenticateAsync() → GoogleWebAuthorizationBroker.AuthorizeAsync() [opens browser] → SDK exchanges code for tokens → DpapiTokenStorageService.StoreTokenAsync → Config.Upsert("GcalTokenResponse", encryptedBase64) → WeakReferenceMessenger.Send(AuthenticationSucceededMessage) → UI shows "Connected".

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Auth initiated: Information "Google Calendar auth initiated". Auth succeeded: Information "Google Calendar auth succeeded for {AccountEmail}". Auth failed: Error "Google Calendar auth failed: {Error}". audit_log entries: gcal_auth on connect, gcal_revoke on disconnect — no sensitive data logged.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: R2: If client_secret.json missing, show setup dialog pointing to README — do not crash. R4: If DPAPI decryption fails (machine migration), log Error and prompt reconnect — no data loss, calendar data remains in Google. Both handled by returning OperationResult.Failure from relevant methods.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Google.Apis.Calendar.v3 1.73.0.3993, Google.Apis.Auth 1.73.0.x (OAuth 2.0 flow, token management, PKCE), Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Polly 9.0.x (retry policies). CommunityToolkit.Mvvm 8.x is also required for WeakReferenceMessenger and relay commands but is NOT yet in the csproj — must be added.

doc.title: Architecture — Google Calendar Management
doc.snippet: Graceful error handling with Serilog structured logging and retry policies (Polly). All service methods return OperationResult<T> — exceptions are caught internally and converted to failure results. Stack traces are never shown to end users.

doc.title: Story 1.6: Implement Application Logging and Error Handling Infrastructure
doc.snippet: services.AddSerilog() requires Serilog.Extensions.Hosting (not installed) — use services.AddLogging(builder => builder.AddSerilog()) instead. Serilog.Sinks.Console 6.1.0 used (6.0.1 unavailable). WriteTo.TextWriter removed from Serilog 4.x — use InMemorySink in tests. ErrorHandlingService.SetWindow(Window) called after window.Activate().

file.reason: Primary data access class. DpapiTokenStorageService injects this to upsert/delete Config rows for token storage. Use CalendarDbContext.Configs DbSet with ConfigKey = "GcalTokenResponse".

Key-value entity used as token storage. Tech spec references "AppMetadata" table but this project uses Config entity instead. Properties: ConfigKey (PK string), ConfigValue (string?), ConfigType (string?), Description (string?), UpdatedAt (DateTime). Store DPAPI-encrypted Base64 token in ConfigValue where ConfigKey = "GcalTokenResponse".

Used to record gcal_auth and gcal_revoke operations. Properties: LogId, Timestamp, OperationType, OperationDetails, AffectedDates, AffectedEvents, UserAction, Success, ErrorMessage. Set OperationType = "gcal_auth" on connect; "gcal_revoke" on disconnect.

Context for Story 2.2 dependency awareness. IMPORTANT: Does not have a sync_token column yet — this is Risk R5 from the tech spec. Story 2.1 does not write to this table; noting for Story 2.2 awareness.

DI registration pattern to follow. Uses services.AddLogging(builder => builder.AddSerilog()), AddSingleton, AddScoped. New services for Story 2.1 must be registered here: ITokenStorageService (Singleton), IGoogleCalendarService (Singleton). ContentDialog XamlRoot pattern: dialog.XamlRoot = window.Content.XamlRoot.
Existing Singleton service. Do not recreate. Pattern for interface definition in this project (namespace GoogleCalendarManagement.Services).
Existing Singleton service. Do not recreate. Available for escalating critical errors from GoogleCalendarService if needed.
Reference implementation for a service that injects CalendarDbContext (Scoped) and ILogger<T>. Follow same constructor injection pattern for GoogleCalendarService and DpapiTokenStorageService.

Reference for unit test patterns. Shows InMemorySink usage for capturing Serilog output (WriteTo.TextWriter is removed in Serilog 4.x). IDisposable pattern for test cleanup. Follow same structure for AuthenticationTests.cs.
Reference for integration test patterns using in-memory SQLite or real DB. Shows how to construct CalendarDbContext for testing without the full DI stack.
EF Core fluent configuration for the Config entity. Review to understand primary key and any existing constraints before upsert logic in DpapiTokenStorageService.

package.name: Google.Apis.Auth
package.note: OAuth 2.0 flow, token management, PKCE enforced by SDK

package.name: CommunityToolkit.Mvvm
package.note: WeakReferenceMessenger, ObservableObject, RelayCommand — NOT yet in csproj, must be added

package.name: Google.Apis.Auth
package.note: Required to mock GoogleWebAuthorizationBroker in unit tests

constraints.constraint: There is NO AppMetadata entity or DbSet in this project. The Config entity (CalendarDbContext.Configs) is the key-value store. Store the DPAPI-encrypted token as ConfigValue where ConfigKey = "GcalTokenResponse". Do NOT create a new AppMetadata entity — use Config as the tech spec's "AppMetadata" equivalent.
Request scope https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar (read + write) — even though Epic 2 only reads. Pre-provisioning the write scope avoids a second consent prompt for Epic 6 (event publishing).

Use ILogger<T> via constructor DI injection — NOT Log.Logger directly. Log.Logger is reserved for App.xaml.cs startup code where DI is not yet available. All services use ILogger<GoogleCalendarService> etc.

Register new services as Singleton in App.xaml.cs ConfigureServices: ITokenStorageService → DpapiTokenStorageService, IGoogleCalendarService → GoogleCalendarService. Follow existing pattern: services.AddLogging(builder => builder.AddSerilog()) — do NOT use services.AddSerilog().

All IGoogleCalendarService methods return OperationResult<T> — never propagate exceptions to callers. Stack traces must never appear in UI. User-facing messages are hardcoded friendly strings, not ex.Message.
IsAuthenticatedAsync() is called on app startup. Must complete in < 200ms (NFR-P1). Load token from Config table (fast SQLite read), check expiry in-memory. No network call on startup.

client_secret.json must NOT be committed to source control. Runtime path: %LOCALAPPDATA%\GoogleCalendarManagement\credentials\client_secret.json. If missing: return OperationResult.Failure with setup message, do not crash. Verify .gitignore covers this path.

If ProtectedData.Unprotect throws CryptographicException (machine migration scenario), catch it, log Error, return null from LoadTokenAsync. IsAuthenticatedAsync returns false → UI shows "Not connected" → user manually reconnects. No app crash. No data loss (calendar data is in Google).

Services placed in root Services/ folder (not a separate Core class library). Epic 1 established this convention. Do NOT create a new GoogleCalendarManagement.Core project — follow existing flat structure. Namespace: GoogleCalendarManagement.Services.

CommunityToolkit.Mvvm 8.x is NOT currently in GoogleCalendarManagement.csproj. Must be added to use WeakReferenceMessenger and ObservableObject/RelayCommand in SettingsViewModel. Add to csproj in Task 1.

ContentDialog XamlRoot must be set before ShowAsync(). Pattern from App.xaml.cs: dialog.XamlRoot = window.Content.XamlRoot. In SettingsViewModel, obtain XamlRoot from the active window's content, not from a static reference.

DataSourceRefresh entity does NOT have a sync_token column yet. This is Risk R5 from tech spec. Story 2.1 does NOT write to DataSourceRefresh — this is Story 2.2's concern. Do not add the column in this story; leave a note for Story 2.2.

interface.name: IGoogleCalendarService
interface.kind: C# interface — to be created in this story

interface.name: ITokenStorageService
interface.kind: C# interface — to be created in this story

interface.name: OperationResult<T>
interface.kind: C# record — to be created in this story

interface.name: OAuthStatus
interface.kind: C# enum — to be created in this story

interface.name: Config (existing entity)
interface.kind: EF Core entity — token storage via CalendarDbContext.Configs

interface.name: AuditLog (existing entity)
interface.kind: EF Core entity — audit trail via CalendarDbContext.AuditLogs

interface.name: GoogleWebAuthorizationBroker (Google SDK)

tests.standards: Test framework: xUnit 2.9.2 with Moq 4.20.72 and FluentAssertions 8.8.0. Unit tests use mock dependencies via Moq. Integration tests use real in-memory SQLite (not mocked). Tests in GoogleCalendarManagement.Tests/Unit/ for unit; Integration/ for integration. All tests must be platform-aware: project built with Platform=x64 and RuntimeIdentifier=win-x64 per test project csproj. DPAPI (ProtectedData) only works on Windows — unit tests for DpapiTokenStorageService will run correctly in CI if the agent is Windows. For cross-platform safety, inject an ITokenStorageService abstraction so non-DPAPI implementations can substitute in tests. Note: WriteTo.TextWriter removed from Serilog 4.x — use custom InMemorySink if log capture needed. Current test count baseline: 24 tests passing.

ideas.test: Mock CalendarDbContext (or use in-memory SQLite). Call StoreTokenAsync with a sample TokenResponse. Assert the ConfigValue stored in Configs table is NOT the raw JSON string — it must be Base64 ciphertext that cannot be directly deserialized as TokenResponse JSON.
Store a TokenResponse with known AccessToken value. Call LoadTokenAsync. Assert returned TokenResponse.AccessToken equals the original. Validates full DPAPI encrypt/decrypt round-trip.
After StoreTokenAsync, call ClearTokenAsync. Assert subsequent LoadTokenAsync returns null. Assert Configs table has no row with ConfigKey = "GcalTokenResponse".

Mock/stub GoogleWebAuthorizationBroker to throw OperationCanceledException. Call AuthenticateAsync. Assert OperationResult.Success == false and ErrorMessage contains "cancelled". Assert no Config row was written (no partial token state persisted).
Point credentials path to a non-existent file path. Call AuthenticateAsync. Assert OperationResult.Success == false and ErrorMessage contains "client_secret.json".
Mock ITokenStorageService.LoadTokenAsync() to return null. Call IsAuthenticatedAsync. Assert OperationResult.Success == true (no error), Data == false (not authenticated).

Mock DpapiTokenStorageService to throw CryptographicException during LoadTokenAsync. Verify IsAuthenticatedAsync returns false without propagating exception. Verify error was logged at Error level (via InMemorySink if log capture needed).
